rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by resolving undefined identifiers or invalid types in the same annex scope where they are referenced.
Do not introduce new package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the AADL model.
When AGREE reports 'named thing must be an expression with a type', trace and correct the first unresolved reference on the same line.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to avoid resolution errors.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE expressions: numeric variables must have numeric expressions, Booleans must have Boolean expressions.
Move implementation-level bindings to implementation scopes; do not place guarantees in component type annexes referencing implementation details.
Delete invalid AGREE clauses instead of adding architecture when identifiers are unresolved or types are mismatched.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use `assign output = expression;` only for valid output features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation scope.
Constants and equations in AGREE must use valid scalar types: int, real, bool.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to resolve missing identifiers by inventing new declarations or modifying unrelated architecture.
Avoid placing implementation-level references in component type annexes; move valid bindings to implementation scope instead.
